deep_snippets.text: * Mechanical energy is the sum of potential energy and kinetic energy ( ). ** Percent efficiency is equal to the mechanical energy at a given position divided by the initial amount of mechanical energy, multiplied by 100 percent. At the top of the ramp, the marble has only gravitational potential energy, so the initial mechanical energy is equal to the gravitational potential energy at the top of the ramp. Therefore, efficiency can be calculated with the equation . > **[Figure 94, Page 80]** A large illustration or diagram (1423×702px) related to: Table, Kinetic, Energy, Position, Mass, Velocity. This image appears on page 80 of the textbook. ![Figure 94 - A large illustration or diagram (1423×702px) related to: Tab](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_LabManual_images/img_0094_5540b1c8.png) Assessments 1. Jack and Jill climb a hill that is 200 m tall. TEKS (6)(B), TEKS (6)(D) a. If Jack has a mass of 65 kg, how many joules of gravitational potential energy does he gain on the climb? b. Jill weighs less than Jack. How will her change in gravitational potential energy compare to Jack’s? Why? 2. A student determines that a block has 100 J of gravitational potential energy at the top of a ramp. He lets the block slide down the ramp and determines that it has 87 J of kinetic energy at the bottom of the ramp. How much mechanical energy is lost? TEKS

Figure 7.1: As a bike coasts downhill, gravitational potential energy is converted to kinetic energy. Mechanical energy is the sum of these two forms of energy. Efficiency is a measure of how much energy is retained as mechanical energy. As efficiency increases, so does the work that is done by the system. Efficiency is usually calculated by dividing the work output ( W O ) of a system by the work input ( W I ), where work input is the initial amount of mechanical energy and work output is the amount of mechanical energy retained after work is done. Efficiency is typically reported as a percentage, written as . In this lab you will build a ramp and calculate the potential energy, kinetic energy, and mechanical energy of a marble at various points as it rolls down the ramp. From your calculations, you will determine the efficiency of this mechanical system. Safety Precautions ● Be careful not to leave marbles lying on the floor where people might step on them and slip.
> **[Figure 91, Page 74]** A large illustration or diagram (974×556px) related to: Activity, Conservation, Energy, TEKS, mechanical, system. This image appears on page 74 of the textbook.
![Figure 91 - A large illustration or diagram (974×556px) related to: Acti](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_LabManual_images/img_0091_a89b84b5.jpg)
For this activity you will need the following: ● Two meter sticks ● 2-inch-wide masking tape ● Small binder clip ● 10 identical marbles ● Level ● Stopwatch, precise to 0.01 seconds ● Marking pen ● Ruler ● Balance ● String

This simulation (http://phet.colorado.edu/en/simulation/energy-skate-park-basics (http://phet.colorado.edu/en/ simulation/energy-skate-park-basics) ) shows how kinetic and potential energy are related, in a scenario similar to the roller coaster. Observe the changes in KE and PE by clicking on the bar graph boxes. Also try the three differently shaped skate parks. Drag the skater to the track to start the animation. The bar graphs show how KE and PE are transformed back and forth. Which statement best explains what happens to the mechanical energy of the system as speed is increasing? a. The mechanical energy of the system increases, provided there is no loss of energy due to friction. The energy would transform to kinetic energy when the speed is increasing. b. The mechanical energy of the system remains constant provided there is no loss of energy due to friction. The energy would transform to kinetic energy when the speed is increasing. c. The mechanical energy of the system increases provided there is no loss of energy due to friction. The energy would transform to potential energy when the speed is increasing. d. The mechanical energy of the system remains constant provided there is no loss of energy due to friction. The energy would transform to potential energy when the speed is increasing.
> **[Figure 177, Page 300]** A wide diagram or timeline (731×275px) related to: Figure, roller, coaster, ride, conversions, potential. This image appears on page 300 of the textbook.
![Figure 177 - A wide diagram or timeline (731×275px) related to: Figure, r](OpenStax_HighSchool_P

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 机械能守恒定律

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 物理
- **学段**: 高中
- **年级**: 高中10年级
- **章节**: 功和能
- **知识点**: 机械能守恒定律
- **课标版本**: 普通高中课程标准（2017年版2020年修订）

---

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> （物理 10年级）机械能守恒定律的内容要求。学生应理解机械能守恒定律的基本概念，掌握基本方法，能运用所学知识解决简单的实际问题。

### 1.2 学业要求
> 学生能准确描述机械能守恒定律的相关概念，能进行基本的运算和推理，能解决与机械能守恒定律相关的实际问题，形成初步的物理思维和素养。

### 1.3 教学提示
> 教学时应注重从学生熟悉的生活情境入手，引导学生通过具体操作、观察、归纳等方式理解机械能守恒定律的概念。注重知识的发生发展过程，帮助学生建立知识之间的联系。

---

## 二、教学目标

### 2.1 知识与技能
- 学生能够理解机械能守恒定律的基本概念和原理
- 学生能够正确进行相关计算和推理
- 学生能够识别和描述相关的概念、现象或材料
- 学生能够理解机械能守恒定律的基本概念和原理

### 2.2 过程与方法
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习物理的方法
- 学生能够运用物理学科方法解决问题
- 学生能够在交流中表达自己的思路
- 学生经历通过自主探究和合作交流，掌握学习物理的方法

### 2.3 情感态度与价值观
- 学生感受物理与现实生活密切相关
- 学生体验物理探究的乐趣，树立学好物理的信心
- 学生养成认真细致、独立思考的学习习惯
- 学生感受物理与现实生活密切相关

---

## 三、教学重难点

### 3.1 教学重点
- 机械能守恒定律的基本内容和基本方法
- 形成完整的知识体系结构

- 建立知识之间的联系，实现知识的迁移应用

Jack and Jill climb a hill that is 200 m tall. TEKS (6)(B), TEKS (6)(D) a. If Jack has a mass of 65 kg, how many joules of gravitational potential energy does he gain on the climb? b. Figure 7.1: As a bike coasts downhill, gravitational potential energy is converted to kinetic energy. Mechanical energy is the sum of these two forms of energy. Efficiency is a measure of how much energy is retained as mechanical energy. As efficiency increases, so does the work that is done by the system. Efficiency is usually calculated by dividing the work output ( W O ) of a system by the work input ( W I ), where work input is the initial amount of mechanical energy and work output is the amount of mechanical energy retained after work is done. Efficiency is typically reported as a percentage, written as . In this lab you will build a ramp and calculate the potential energy, kinetic energy, and mechanical energy of a marble at various points as it rolls down the ramp. > **[Figure 90, Page 72]** A large illustration or diagram (1423×702px) related to: Work, Energy, done, force, causes, object. This image appears on page 72 of the textbook.
![Figure 90 - A large illustration or diagram (1423×702px) related to: Wor](OpenStax_HighSchool_Physics_LabManual_images/img_0090_5540b1c8.png)
In this lab, you will explore the relationship between work and energy. In Activity 1, you will measure the change in kinetic and potential energy of a marble as it rolls down a ramp. In Activity 2, you will use a simple machine called a lever to explore the relationship between work, force, and distance. In Activity 3, you will run up a staircase and calculate how much work you do per unit time. Work done per unit time is called power . In this lab you will learn: ● the nature of kinetic-energy and potential-energy transformations; ● how a simple machine is able to reduce force while conserving energy; ● how to increase power without increasing the amount of work done.
Activity 1: Conservation of Energy TEKS (6)(B), TEKS (6)(D) In a mechanical system, potential energy is converted to kinetic energy, and kinetic energy is converted to potential energy. In a mechanical system, mechanical energy ( ME ) is the sum of potential energy and kinetic energy, as indicated by the equation . In Figure 7.1, all of the bicycle’s potential energy is converted to kinetic energy as the bicycle rolls down the hill, and so the total mechanical energy of the system remains constant. In real life, however, some energy is always lost as heat. For example, as the bicycle's tires roll

exercises.stem: 依据课标「机械能守恒定律」：通过实验，验证机械能守恒定律。理解机械能守恒定律，体会守恒观念对认识物理规律的重要性。 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：能用机械能守恒定律分析生产生活中的有关问题。
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察家用电器、交通工具、运动器材或自然现象中与「机械能守恒定律」相关的物理规律。
